title: podman on arm: `Error: failed to open 2048 locks in /libpod_lock: numerical result out of range`
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Another one for edge-cases-no-sane-person-is-ever-likely-to-encounter: `arm` userland but running on a 64bit kernel executing an `arm64` binary (with appropriate libraries available in `/lib64`/`/usr/lib64`):

Any podman command (`podman image ls`, `podman ps`, even `podman info`) results in:

```
Error: failed to open 2048 locks in /libpod_lock: numerical result out of range
```

This doesn't occur when running a 32bit binary - so I'm adding this more as a note of curious behaviour than anything else.  Note, however, that the 32bit binary is current unusable due to https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/575, and `runc` reporting `container exited on bad system call` :(

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Error: failed to open 2048 locks in /libpod_lock: numerical result out of range
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
Error: failed to open 2048 locks in /libpod_lock: numerical result out of range
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body:
Are you, by chance, swapping between 32 and 64 bit binaries on this system?
If so, you may want to try ‘podman system renumber’. I think all the locks
code uses fixed size integers, but maybe an int snuck in somewhere and it’s
confusing the matter.

On Sat, Jan 30, 2021 at 09:46 Stuart Shelton <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> *Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)*
>
> /kind bug
>
> *Description*
>
> Another one for edge-cases-no-sane-person-is-ever-likely-to-encounter: arm
> userland but running on a 64bit kernel executing an arm64 binary (with
> appropriate libraries available in /lib64//usr/lib64):
>
> Any podman command (podman image ls, podman ps, even podman info) results
> in:
>
> Error: failed to open 2048 locks in /libpod_lock: numerical result out of range
>
> This doesn't occur when running a 32bit binary - so I'm adding this more
> as a note of curious behaviour than anything else. Note, however, that the
> 32bit binary is current unusable due to containers/crun#575
> <https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/575>, and podman doesn't seem
> to pass-through BUILDAH_RUNTIME to buildah :(
>
> *Output of podman version:*
>
> Error: failed to open 2048 locks in /libpod_lock: numerical result out of range
>
> *Output of podman info --debug:*
>
> Error: failed to open 2048 locks in /libpod_lock: numerical result out of range
>
> *Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked
> the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?*
>
> Yes
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9164>, or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCD524LC7WYAHPCKD3LS4QLVJANCNFSM4W2LZWQA>
> .
>

You could be onto something there, but sadly with a 64bit binary:

```
$ sudo podman system renumber
Error: failed to open 2048 locks in /libpod_lock: numerical result out of range
```

... what can I delete on-disk to remove whatever locks need re-numbering?

Also, could `podman` not detect this particular error and automatically run a `system renumber` and try the operation again?

(... and, if it is the right response, could `system renumber` be made not to abort if a system renumber is required?! ;)

The locks file is in `/dev/shm` - if you have no Podman processes running (OK to have containers running, as long as you're not attached to them) then it's safe to remove anything in `/dev/shm` with a name starting with `libpod`.

`system renumber` is a somewhat involved operation under the hood (can potentially do bad things if other Podman processes are running at the same time) so we can't really automate it safely.

Perfect!  I'd cleared the entries under `(/var)/run` and even the storage directory, but didn't think to check `/dev/shm`... and with `/dev/shm/libpod_lock` removed, it looks as if things are working again!
So this is not an issue, for us to fix, I am closing.  If I am mistaken please reopen.

> So this is not an issue, for us to fix, I am closing. If I am mistaken please reopen.

Not worth re-opening this issue for, but perhaps a clearer error message which at least mentions `podman system renumber` might give any user hitting this a clearer indication of how to solve the problem?
Just experienced this with Fedora 36 after getting the latest podman.  FWIW, podman system renumber doesn't remove the file in /dev/shm and so I couldn't fix the issue until I found this thread.
